## 1. dora-metrics.md

**Hash:** 9f4b2c8e1a7d3f5b9c2e4f8a1b3d5e7f9c1a2b4d6e8f0a2c4e6f8a0c2e4f6a8c
**Terms:** DORA, deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, mean time to restore, MTTR, cycle time, elite performers, high performers, low performers, State of DevOps Report, Forsgren, Humble, Kim, Accelerate, deployment metrics, four key metrics
**Facts:**
- Elite teams deploy on demand with cycle time <1 hour, n=39000 (DORA 2024)
- Change failure rate for elite teams is 0-15%; low performers exceed 60% (DORA 2024)
- MTTR correlates strongly with deployment frequency at r=0.78 (Forsgren et al. 2018, Accelerate)
- Teams using all four DORA metrics improve deployment frequency 2.4x year-over-year (DORA 2024 longitudinal subset, n=4200)
**Links:** continuous-delivery-evidence.md, trunk-based-development.md, programme-issue-#42

## 2. tdd-effectiveness.md

**Hash:** 3e7a9c1b5d8f2a4c6e8b0d2f4a6c8e0b2d4f6a8c0e2b4d6f8a0c2e4f6a8c0e2b
**Terms:** test-driven development, TDD, red-green-refactor, defect density, Madeyski, IBM Research, branch coverage, mutation score, unit testing, Beck, test-first
**Facts:**
- TDD reduces defect density by 40-50% (Madeyski 2010, IEEE TSE, controlled study)
- TDD increases branch coverage by 20-30% over test-after (Madeyski 2010, n=188 student projects)
- IBM Drivers of Practice study shows TDD reduces defect rates by ~50% on production code (IBM Research 2005)
- Mutation score improves 20-30% with TDD vs test-after (multiple studies, meta-analysis 2015)
**Links:** dora-metrics.md, continuous-delivery-evidence.md, ADR-007-testing-strategy

## 3. tla-plus-formal-verification.md

**Hash:** 8c2e4f6a0b2d4f6a8c0e2b4d6f8a0c2e4f6a8c0e2b4d6f8a0c2e4f6a8c0e2b4d
**Terms:** TLA+, formal verification, model checking, TLC, Lamport, distributed systems, S3, DynamoDB, Cosmos DB, Cloudflare, AWS, specifications, invariants, safety properties, liveness, temporal logic
**Facts:**
- TLA+ caught 6 critical bugs in DynamoDB before launch (AWS 2018, "How Amazon Web Services Uses Formal Methods")
- Azure Cosmos DB used TLA+ to verify consistency model across 5 levels (Microsoft 2018)
- Cloudflare uses TLA+ for distributed cache coherency protocols since 2019 (Cloudflare engineering blog)
- Specification effort: ~4 weeks per system; defect cost averted: estimated 6-12 months of debugging (AWS 2018)
**Links:** assured-sdlc-pattern.md, distributed-systems-correctness.md, ADR-014-consistency-model
